title: excoecaria biglandulosa var. glandulata
intro: Excoecaria biglandulosa var. glandulata, also known as the poison tree, is a toxic plant native to Australia, characterized by its bright yellow sap. Traditionally, its sap has been used to treat various ailments, exhibiting medicinal properties such as anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Excoecaria biglandulosa var. glandulata include its ability to reduce pain, treat skin conditions like eczema, and alleviate symptoms of fever. In herbal preparations, the sap is often used to create ointments, creams, and tinctures, requiring careful handling and processing due to its toxicity.
